summary.intent: Verify all PR review comments on moltis PR #439 (fix-mcp-naming branch) were addressed, resolve the review threads, and ensure CI passes.
summary.outcome: All 7 review threads resolved; biome formatting errors fixed using the correct project-local biome 2.4.6 binary (not npx's stale 0.3.3); changes committed and pushed.

repo: The project uses biome 2.4.6 managed via mise — running `npx biome` resolves an ancient 0.3.3 version and produces incorrect results; the correct binary is at ~/.local/share/mise/installs/biome/2.4.6/bin/biome or equivalent.
Local validation is run via a script that checks fmt (cargo), biome (JS), i18n parity, and zizmor (GitHub Actions security); all must pass before pushing.

code.finding: Same biome rule: `${editTransport.value === 'sse' && html`...`}` must be split across lines.
Idiomatic display_name parsing uses a clean match: `Some(v) if v.is_null() => None, Some(v) => v.as_str().map(String::from), None => existing...`

workflow: When biome reports formatting errors in CI, always use the project-local biome binary rather than npx to avoid version mismatches.
GitHub review threads must be explicitly resolved via API even after the underlying code is fixed — they don't auto-resolve.

friction: npx biome resolved version 0.3.3 instead of the project's 2.4.6, causing `biome check --write` to silently do nothing and report a false pass.
The GH_TOKEN lacked commit status write permission, causing status publication failures during local validation (non-blocking but noisy).
Multiple Read tool calls were needed to load the full JS file due to size, slowing initial analysis.
open_items: The redundant `params.get()` pattern for `url` at lines 119-130 in mcp_service.rs (triple calls) predates this PR and was not addressed — potential future cleanup.
GH_TOKEN permissions should be updated to include `repo:status` write access to avoid status publication failures in local CI.
